data_flow_trace.original: StakingConfig.sol:89-149 does NOT call _requireInitialized() at setters, unlike peer runtime configs
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: StakingConfig has been refactored to the pending-config pattern. There is a single GOVERNANCE entry point `setForNextEpoch(_minimumStake,_lockupDurationMicros,_unbondingDelayMicros)` at lines 130-147, which calls `requireAllowed(GOVERNANCE)` (L135) and then `_requireInitialized()` at L136 before `_validateConfig` and writing `_pendingConfig`.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: Caller must be GOVERNANCE and contract must already be initialized (_initialized == true).
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: The four individual setters named in the issue (setMinimumStake, setLockupDurationMicros, setUnbondingDelayMicros, setMinimumProposalStake) no longer exist in the file; grep across src/ returns zero matches. A single batched `setForNextEpoch` now exists and DOES call `_requireInitialized()`.

data_flow_trace.original: A GOVERNANCE-authorised caller invoking setLockupDurationMicros before Genesis completes would silently write the new value and emit ConfigUpdated with oldValue=0
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: Any GOVERNANCE invocation of `setForNextEpoch` prior to `initialize()` reverts with `Errors.StakingConfigNotInitialized()` because `_requireInitialized()` (L198-200) checks `_initialized` which is only set true inside `initialize()` at L98.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: `_initialized` flag must be true; otherwise revert.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Pre-init GOVERNANCE writes are now hard-reverted, matching peer configs (ValidatorConfig:162, EpochConfig:88, ConsensusConfig:81, ExecutionConfig:79, GovernanceConfig:111, VersionConfig:85, RandomnessConfig:112).

data_flow_trace.original: Genesis.initialize calling StakingConfig.initialize later overwrites the previously GOVERNANCE-set values, silently losing the write
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: initialize() is GENESIS-only (requireAllowed at L91), reverts on re-entry via `AlreadyInitialized` check at L92, and now no pre-init GOVERNANCE write path exists to be overwritten, because setForNextEpoch reverts pre-init.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: The ordering race described (pre-Genesis GOVERNANCE write then later overwrite) cannot arise: the GOVERNANCE path is gated on _initialized.

data_flow_trace.original: Guard-consistency gap on 4 setters: setMinimumStake, setLockupDurationMicros, setUnbondingDelayMicros, setMinimumProposalStake
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: Those 4 named setters are gone (grep across src/ returns 0 matches). The replacement single setter `setForNextEpoch` is guard-consistent with all peer configs: first requireAllowed(GOVERNANCE), then _requireInitialized(), then _validateConfig. `minimumProposalStake` has been deprecated (see `__deprecated_minimumProposalStake` storage gap at L50-53).
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: The divergence cited is eliminated; StakingConfig now matches the pending-config pattern and guard shape of its peers.

reasoning_chain: The issue alleges that four individual GOVERNANCE setters in StakingConfig.sol skip `_requireInitialized()`, enabling silent pre-Genesis writes that diverge from peer runtime configs. In current code at commit a623eab, those individual setters do not exist: `grep -r 'setLockupDurationMicros|setMinimumStake|setUnbondingDelayMicros|setMinimumProposalStake' src/` returns zero matches. StakingConfig was refactored (commits 49dce4a, d3df269 — 'refactor: apply pending config pattern to StakingConfig (GCC-004)') to use the same pending-config pattern as its peers: a single `setForNextEpoch(...)` that calls `requireAllowed(GOVERNANCE)` then `_requireInitialized()` (L135-136), and `applyPendingConfig()` that also calls `_requireInitialized()` (L158). `minimumProposalStake` was removed entirely, leaving only a storage gap (L50-53). The attack path at every step is broken: any pre-init GOVERNANCE call reverts with `StakingConfigNotInitialized`. Guard shape is now consistent with ValidatorConfig, EpochConfig, ConsensusConfig, ExecutionConfig, GovernanceConfig, VersionConfig, RandomnessConfig.

key_evidence.excerpt: function setForNextEpoch(uint256 _minimumStake, uint64 _lockupDurationMicros, uint64 _unbondingDelayMicros) external { requireAllowed(SystemAddresses.GOVERNANCE); _requireInitialized(); _validateConfig(...); _pendingConfig = PendingConfig({...}); hasPendingConfig = true; emit PendingStakingConfigSet(); }
key_evidence.why_matters: This is the sole GOVERNANCE-callable setter now; it explicitly invokes _requireInitialized() at L136, closing the exact gap alleged.

key_evidence.excerpt: function applyPendingConfig() external { requireAllowed(SystemAddresses.RECONFIGURATION); _requireInitialized(); if (!hasPendingConfig) return; minimumStake = _pendingConfig.minimumStake; lockupDurationMicros = _pendingConfig.lockupDurationMicros; unbondingDelayMicros = _pendingConfig.unbondingDelayMicros; ... }
key_evidence.why_matters: Epoch-boundary application also requires initialization (L158), fully matching peer applyPendingConfig semantics.

key_evidence.excerpt: function _requireInitialized() internal view { if (!_initialized) revert Errors.StakingConfigNotInitialized(); }
key_evidence.why_matters: Dedicated guard that reverts pre-Genesis governance writes — identical pattern to peers.

key_evidence.excerpt: /// @notice DEPRECATED: kept as storage gap... uint256 private __deprecated_minimumProposalStake;
key_evidence.why_matters: setMinimumProposalStake removal is intentional — the field was deprecated, so the fourth setter cited in the issue has no replacement and cannot be exploited.

counterargument: One could argue that the removal of individual setters is a side-effect refactor and the underlying guard-consistency claim was originally valid at an earlier commit, making the finding 'fixed' rather than 'never existed'. However, the issue asks whether the vulnerability currently exists; it does not. Additionally, one might claim that some other code path writes staking parameters pre-init — but a full grep for writes to minimumStake/lockupDurationMicros/unbondingDelayMicros outside StakingConfig.sol returns no other contracts mutating these fields, and initialize() itself is GENESIS-only and one-shot (`if (_initialized) revert AlreadyInitialized;` at L92). So the residual pre-init write surface is zero.
